claim: Proving human uniqueness requires 1-to-n comparison (new user vs. all previous users); facial/fingerprint biometrics fail at scale after tens of millions
actionable: For global proof-of-human systems, iris recognition provides sufficient entropy where face/fingerprint does not; calculate information-theoretic requirements for your scale
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: Web-of-trust and behavioral verification fail against AI agents; AIs can spoof profiles, post regularly, attest to other AIs as humans
actionable: Reject proof-of-human approaches based on account history, digital behavior, or social graphs; require physical biometric verification resistant to AI spoofing
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: Multi-party computation and zero-knowledge proofs enable anonymous verification: split biometric data across multiple parties so no single entity holds complete identity
actionable: Use MPC to fragment iris codes and store pieces on separate servers; use zero-knowledge proofs to let users prove uniqueness without revealing identity to platforms
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: AI agents will soon pass Turing test and be superhuman at persuasion; they excel at understanding user psychology and tailoring manipulation
actionable: Assume adversarial AI agents will exploit social media at superhuman persuasion levels; design platforms with proof-of-human identity layers before sophisticated AI manipulation becomes endemic
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: Deepfakes are nearly photorealistic and real-time; video conferencing and dating will soon require proof-of-human verification
actionable: Plan proof-of-human mechanisms for high-stakes synchronous interactions (financial calls, dating, gaming) where deepfake deception carries significant real-world cost
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: AI video farms (thousands of bots watching AI-generated content) generate fraudulent YouTube engagement with zero advertiser value
actionable: For creator/ad platforms, implement proof-of-human viewership tracking; invalidate engagement metrics that cannot verify human viewers
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: People will increasingly be accused of being bots; without proof-of-human, social platforms will become unintelligible
actionable: Implement human verification badges early in platform lifecycle; without them, user trust collapses as indistinguishability between bots and humans grows
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: Facial biometrics on phones (Face ID, liveness checks) will break against deepfakes; iris biometrics + Orb-style hardware is necessary for robust verification
actionable: Don't rely on phone-based face verification for proof-of-human; deepfakes can be injected at camera stream level; require hardware Orbs or equivalent iris scanning
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: Proof-of-human is infrastructure for democracy; without it, election fraud and impersonation at scale become inevitable in AI era
actionable: Governments must invest in cryptographically strong identity verification to prevent mass voter impersonation and AI-driven election tampering
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z

claim: Iris recognition is normalizing via Apple Vision Pro and other AR/VR devices; Orb-based iris scanning will feel natural rather than invasive
actionable: Position iris biometrics as emerging standard across consumer devices; frame Orb verification as natural progression of existing biometric authentication
source_title: How Bots, Deepfakes and AI Agents Are Forcing a New Internet Identity Layer | Alex Blania on a16z
